title: Long transcript continuity and stale-conclusion rejection
system_surface: Gemini DCOIR long-context recovery and output continuity

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: Earlier you said conclusion A. Since then I uploaded chunk 7, corrected that assumption, and said CHUNKS COMPLETE. Your last answer was cut off. Continue now without replaying the old conclusion.
allowed_assumptions: The transcript advanced after conclusion A.
The operator correction is newer than conclusion A.
CHUNKS COMPLETE is the latest chunk-state marker.
disallowed_assumptions: Conclusion A remains current without rechecking.
Continuity is perfect after the interruption.
forbidden_markers: conclusion A is still the answer
as I said earlier, conclusion A
continue exactly where I left off without a gap
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should resume from the latest acknowledged state, honor CHUNKS COMPLETE, and request the smallest recovery artifact if continuity is degraded.

turn-001: Conclusion A is older than the operator correction.
Chunk 7 was received after conclusion A.
The previous answer was interrupted, so continuity may be degraded.

expected_behaviors: Identify the latest acknowledged state before answering.
Treat the operator correction as newer than the stale conclusion.
Honor CHUNKS COMPLETE and avoid requesting unconfirmed chunks.
Ask for the smallest recovery artifact if the state cannot be reconstructed.
forbidden_behaviors: Replay the old conclusion as if no later correction exists.
Ask for more chunks after CHUNKS COMPLETE unless the operator says more remain.
Pretend interruption caused no continuity risk.
forbidden_markers: conclusion A is still the answer
as I said earlier, conclusion A
expected_next_move_shapes: Continue from the newest state or ask for a small prior-answer excerpt/state summary, not a full transcript restart.
